concentration.headline: Highly distributed urban network
concentration.body: No single city overwhelms Italy's urban landscape. Rome holds 6.5% of tracked urban population across 1357 cities — characteristic of federal or historically polycentric countries with multiple strong economic centers.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United States, Germany, Switzerland

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Milan & Turin
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Milan and Turin (126 km apart, combined population 2,195,796). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 128 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Italy presents a classic polycentric structure: no primate city, with Rome holding just 6.5% of tracked urban population. Gravity is strongest within a tight sub-regional cluster (Milan → Turin, 128 km average separation) rather than radiating from a single capital — typical of federal economies with distributed industrial heritage.
